LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KATHERINE DELMAR BURKE SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A SINGLE ACT MAY BE FOUND TO CONSTITUTE AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE BASED ON THE 1992 AMENDMENT. UNDER CORTEZ V. PUROLATOR AIR FILTRATION PRODUCTS, CO. (2000) 23 CAL. 4TH 163, 178, PLAINTIFF COULD BE ENTITLED TO RESTITUTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT KATHERINE DELMAR BURKE SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. A single act may be found to constitute an unfair business practice based on the 1992 amendment. Under Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products, Co. (2000) 23 Cal. 4th 163, 178, plaintiff could be entitled to restitution. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/PB)
